behaviors.behavior: The footer thunk is not invoked when ordered rules 1-4 match first (5 cases)
behaviors.evidence: server recovery-schedule.test.ts > is NOT paid when rule %s matches first - PASS; mutation M1 (eager scan) fails 11 tests

behaviors.behavior: The footer thunk is invoked exactly once when rules 1-4 all miss
behaviors.evidence: server recovery-schedule.test.ts > is paid EXACTLY ONCE when rules 1-4 all miss - PASS; mutation M2 (consulted twice) FAILS

behaviors.behavior: An INVALID pointer never invokes the thunk
behaviors.evidence: server recovery-schedule.test.ts > is NOT paid for an INVALID pointer - PASS

behaviors.behavior: ScenarioDecision.runIdSource reports the winning rule for all six rules
behaviors.evidence: server recovery-schedule.test.ts > reports WHICH source identified the run - PASS; mutation M9 (source lies) FAILS

behaviors.behavior: A campaign or pipeline session quoting a corroborated footer performs ZERO scans across polls
behaviors.evidence: server recovery-schedule.test.ts Part B, real _recoveryScanCount() over 3 polls - PASS

behaviors.behavior: An unidentified session scans once, recovers, and persists source=transcript_run_id
behaviors.evidence: server recovery-schedule.test.ts Part B + routes.recovery.test.ts association shape, exactly one persist - PASS; mutation M3 (not persisted) FAILS

behaviors.behavior: The wide reach-back happens once for a transcript that never changes
behaviors.evidence: server routes.recovery-schedule.test.ts > reaches back ONCE ... budget sequence - PASS; mutation M4 (never narrows) FAILS

behaviors.behavior: It happens again after the transcript revision moves, and the recovery succeeds
behaviors.evidence: server routes.recovery-schedule.test.ts > reaches back AGAIN ... - PASS; mutation M5 (never repeats) FAILS

behaviors.behavior: A read yielding no revision never marks the reach-back done
behaviors.evidence: server routes.recovery-schedule.test.ts > a read that yields NO revision ... - PASS; mutation M6 FAILS

behaviors.behavior: Cap eviction only fires for a NEW key, so tasks already in the map keep their marker
behaviors.evidence: server routes.recovery-schedule.test.ts > evicting at the cap ... - PASS; mutation M10 (clear on every write) FAILS

behaviors.behavior: A rolled-back association (failed persist) also drops the reach-back marker
behaviors.evidence: server routes.recovery-schedule.test.ts > a ROLLED-BACK association ... - PASS; mutation M12 FAILS

behaviors.behavior: An already-identified task never consumes a cap slot
behaviors.evidence: server routes.recovery-schedule.test.ts > an already-identified task does not consume the cap - PASS; mutation M13 FAILS

behaviors.behavior: An identified task never requests the wide window
behaviors.evidence: server routes.recovery.test.ts > reads the WIDER tail only while unidentified, then drops back - PASS

behaviors.behavior: runLive is documented with the full clause set on BOTH sides of the mirror
behaviors.evidence: server mission-runlive-doc-parity.test.ts - PASS; mutations M7/M11/M14 (mirror doc weakened) FAIL

behaviors.behavior: The two runLive docs cannot agree with each other and both be wrong
behaviors.evidence: server mission-runlive-doc-parity.test.ts > the implementation really is that conjunction - PASS; mutation M8 FAILS

behaviors.behavior: wire.ts returns the tail WITH a revision, end-to-end through SessionWatcher
behaviors.evidence: e2e mission-recovery-schedule.spec.ts (2) + mission-run-identity-recovery.spec.ts (4) - 6 PASS on an isolated stack; PROVED falsifiable by breaking wire.ts to return empty text, which fails the recovered-rail flow

behaviors.behavior: The prior run's record states the persistence half of the (e) reversal
behaviors.evidence: Prose in the prior run's adr.md + decision drop (both gitignored); verified by reading, and restated in this run's commit message so it ships

surface_verification.justification: Backend-affects-Frontend: the diff changes the mission-context route's read schedule and the reader contract it depends on, so surface=web is mandatory even though the response shape is unchanged. Executed client/e2e/flows/mission-recovery-schedule.spec.ts (new, 2) + mission-run-identity-recovery.spec.ts (the 4 flows from PR #309, as the regression gate) in real Chromium against an isolated single-process stack (temp USERPROFILE, SHIPWRIGHT_E2E_ISOLATED=1, built client via SHIPWRIGHT_STATIC_DIR, Hono 127.0.0.1:3853, SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local): 6 passed. Verified LOAD-BEARING by re-running against a deliberately broken wire.ts (tail text returned empty while the revision stayed valid): the recovered-rail flow FAILED as required, and the negative flow correctly stayed green.
